enonce.texte:
Read the sentences below and identify the correct future tense form.

1. I _______ (go) to the university next year.

2. She _______ (become) a doctor. She has already applied to medical school.

3. They _______ (visit) their grandparents next weekend.

4. He _______ (study) medicine because he wants to help people.

5. We _______ (start) a new business in January.

questions.texte: Complete each sentence with the correct future tense.
questions.reponse:
1. will go / am going to go
2. is going to become / will become
3. will visit / are going to visit
4. is going to study
5. will start / are going to start

sousQuestions.texte: Complete sentence 1 with a future tense.
sousQuestions.reponse: I will go / am going to go to the university next year.

sousQuestions.texte: Complete sentence 2 with a future tense indicating a plan.
sousQuestions.reponse: She is going to become a doctor. She has already applied to medical school.

sousQuestions.texte: Complete sentence 3 with a future tense.
sousQuestions.reponse: They will visit / are going to visit their grandparents next weekend.

sousQuestions.texte: Complete sentence 4 with a future tense indicating intention.
sousQuestions.reponse: He is going to study medicine because he wants to help people.

sousQuestions.texte: Complete sentence 5 with a future tense.
sousQuestions.reponse: We will start / are going to start a new business in January.

enonce.texte: Read the situations and write sentences about future plans using will or be going to.

questions.texte: Write sentences expressing future plans for each situation.
questions.reponse: Accept any logical sentences using will or be going to.

sousQuestions.texte: A student has decided to become a journalist.
sousQuestions.reponse: She is going to study journalism at the university. She will become a journalist.

sousQuestions.texte: Someone made a spontaneous decision to help a friend.
sousQuestions.reponse: I will help you with your homework. I'll call you tomorrow.

sousQuestions.texte: A person has a plan to start a business after graduation.
sousQuestions.reponse: He is going to start a business after graduation. He will be an entrepreneur.

sousQuestions.texte: A student has a dream to travel abroad.
sousQuestions.reponse: She is going to travel abroad next year. She will visit several countries.

questions.texte: Match each profession to its correct definition.
questions.reponse:
1. Journalist: A person who reports news
2. Doctor: A person who treats sick people
3. Lawyer: A person who defends people in court
4. Engineer: A person who designs and builds things
5. Teacher: A person who educates students

sousQuestions.texte: A person who reports news.
sousQuestions.reponse: Journalist

sousQuestions.texte: A person who treats sick people.
sousQuestions.reponse: Doctor

sousQuestions.texte: A person who defends people in court.
sousQuestions.reponse: Lawyer

sousQuestions.texte: A person who designs and builds things.
sousQuestions.reponse: Engineer

sousQuestions.texte: A person who educates students.
sousQuestions.reponse: Teacher

intitule: Writing a Letter to a Family Member

enonce.texte:
You have decided to pursue a career in a field that your family may not understand. Write a letter to a family member (parent, uncle, or aunt) explaining your career choice and persuading them of its merits.

Instructions:

  • Explain what career you have chosen
  • Give reasons why you chose it
  • Explain how it will benefit you and society
  • Address any concerns they might have
  • Use future tenses (will, be going to)

questions.texte: Write a letter to a family member explaining your career choice.
questions.reponse: A well-structured letter with clear arguments, using appropriate vocabulary and future tenses.

sousQuestions.texte: Include at least 3 reasons for your career choice.
sousQuestions.reponse: Student provides valid reasons like passion, job opportunities, contribution to society.

sousQuestions.texte: Use at least 5 future tense sentences (will or be going to).
sousQuestions.reponse: Student correctly uses future tenses in their letter.

sousQuestions.texte: Address concerns your family might have.
sousQuestions.reponse: Student acknowledges and responds to potential family concerns.

enonce.texte:
Read the text below and answer the questions.

Choosing a Career

Many young people struggle to choose a career. Some follow their passion, while others choose a path that offers financial security. The best choice is to find a balance between what you love and what can provide a good life. For example, Marie, a student in Yaoundé, has always loved science. She is going to study medicine at the university next year. She will become a doctor and plans to work in rural areas where there are few doctors. She believes that helping others is the best way to contribute to society. On the other hand, Jean wants to become a businessman. He will start a small business after graduation. He is going to sell agricultural products to support local farmers. Both Marie and Jean have clear plans for their future.

questions.texte: Answer the following questions based on the text.
questions.reponse:
1. Marie loves science.
2. She is going to study medicine at university.
3. She will work in rural areas because there are few doctors there.
4. Jean wants to become a businessman.
5. He will sell agricultural products to support local farmers.
6. The text suggests finding a balance between passion and financial security.

sousQuestions.texte: What does Marie love?
sousQuestions.reponse: Marie loves science.

sousQuestions.texte: What is Marie going to study?
sousQuestions.reponse: She is going to study medicine at the university.

sousQuestions.texte: Why will Marie work in rural areas?
sousQuestions.reponse: She will work in rural areas because there are few doctors there.

sousQuestions.texte: What career does Jean want to pursue?
sousQuestions.reponse: Jean wants to become a businessman.

sousQuestions.texte: What will Jean sell?
sousQuestions.reponse: He will sell agricultural products to support local farmers.

sousQuestions.texte: What does the text suggest about choosing a career?
sousQuestions.reponse: The text suggests finding a balance between passion and financial security.

enonce.texte:
Read the two career descriptions below and answer the questions.

Career 1: Teaching - Teachers educate students and help shape the future. They work in schools and universities. They inspire young minds and contribute to society.

Career 2: Engineering - Engineers design and build structures, machines, and systems. They solve problems and create new technologies. They work in construction, manufacturing, and many other fields.

questions.texte: Compare the two careers and answer the following questions.
questions.reponse:
1. Teachers educate students; engineers design and build things.
2. Teachers work in schools; engineers work in construction, manufacturing, etc.
3. Both contribute to society and require specific skills and training.
4. Accept any logical answer.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the main role of a teacher?
sousQuestions.reponse: Teachers educate students and help shape the future.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the main role of an engineer?
sousQuestions.reponse: Engineers design and build structures, machines, and systems.

sousQuestions.texte: How are these two careers similar?
sousQuestions.reponse: Both contribute to society and require specific skills and training.

sousQuestions.texte: Which career would you choose? Explain why.
sousQuestions.reponse: Accept any logical answer explaining the student's preference.

enonce.texte:
Imagine your family wants you to study medicine, but you want to study journalism. Write a letter to your parents to convince them to support your choice. Use persuasive language and explain your reasons clearly.

Include:

  • Your career choice and why you want it
  • Why journalism is important in society
  • How you plan to succeed in this field
  • Address their concerns about job security
  • Use future tenses and persuasive language

questions.texte: Write a persuasive letter to your parents explaining your career choice.
questions.reponse: A well-written persuasive letter with clear arguments, future tenses, and effective persuasion techniques.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why journalism is important in society.
sousQuestions.reponse: Student explains journalism's role in informing the public and holding power accountable.

sousQuestions.texte: Address concerns about job security.
sousQuestions.reponse: Student provides reasons why journalism offers opportunities (digital media, freelancing, etc.).

sousQuestions.texte: Use future tenses to describe your plans.
sousQuestions.reponse: Student uses will/be going to to express their intentions.

sousQuestions.texte: Use persuasive and respectful language.
sousQuestions.reponse: Student uses polite and convincing language to persuade their parents.
